name: brain-mcp
description: A private local long-term memory for Claude. Save, search, version and browse clean markdown notes in your own SQLite store; never leaves your computer unless you opt in.
long_description: Brain gives Claude a persistent personal knowledge base that lives entirely on your machine. Claude writes clean notes (manually with 'save to brain' or automatically once a conversation crosses about ten pages), stores them in a local SQLite database with full-text search, keeps version history, and exposes a localhost web UI so you can browse, edit, restore and export your notes as markdown. Secrets (API keys, tokens, private keys) are stripped before anything is written. No telemetry, no cloud, no account. Optional restructuring engines (host model via MCP sampling, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint including a local Ollama) can be enabled per-user. Uses Node's built-in SQLite, so the one-click install loads on macOS, Windows and Linux with no native build step.

tools.name: brain_save
tools.description: Save a markdown note to long-term memory with a title, optional folder, and tags.

tools.name: brain_search
tools.description: Full-text search across saved notes; returns ids, titles, and snippets.

tools.name: brain_get
tools.description: Fetch one note in full by id.

tools.name: brain_list
tools.description: List recent notes with per-folder counts.

tools.name: brain_versions
tools.description: List the version history of a note.

tools.name: brain_restore
tools.description: Restore a note to an earlier version (non-destructive).

tools.name: brain_capture
tools.description: Append the latest exchange to a per-session buffer for later saving.

tools.name: brain_flush
tools.description: Restructure the running buffer into one note, save it, and clear the buffer.

tools.name: brain_export
tools.description: Export all notes to a zip of folder-structured markdown.

tools.name: brain_import
tools.description: Import notes from a Brain export zip or any zip of markdown files.

tools.name: brain_open_ui
tools.description: Open the local web viewer to browse, edit, restore, and download notes.

tools.name: brain_system_check
tools.description: Report RAM/CPU and recommend a local model and setup for offline restructuring.

restructure_mode.title: Restructure engine
restructure_mode.description: How notes are cleaned up before saving. 'none' stores exactly what was written (recommended). 'sampling' asks the host's own model via MCP sampling. 'openai' uses the OpenAI-compatible endpoint configured below.

brain_dir.title: Storage directory
brain_dir.description: Where the brain.db SQLite file and exports are stored. Leave blank for ~/.brain.

flush_tokens.title: Auto-save threshold (tokens)
flush_tokens.description: Capture buffer size (in tokens) that triggers should_flush. ~6000 tokens is roughly 10 pages.

ui_port.title: Web viewer port
ui_port.description: Localhost port for the built-in browser UI.

llm_base_url.title: LLM base URL (openai mode)
llm_base_url.description: OpenAI-compatible /v1 endpoint. Use https://api.openai.com/v1 for OpenAI, http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1 for Ollama, http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1 for LM Studio.

llm_api_key.title: LLM API key (openai mode)
llm_api_key.description: Leave blank for local servers (Ollama, LM Studio).

llm_model.title: LLM model (openai mode)
llm_model.description: Model id, e.g. gpt-4o-mini, or the local model you pulled in Ollama.
